ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon webshell execution: w3wp.exe spawning shells / recon / credential-dumping LOLBins on Exchange
ucs.description: Catches the signature post-exploitation chain that defined HAFNIUM's Operation Exchange Marauder (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and remains their on-prem Exchange playbook: a China-Chopper-style .aspx webshell hosted under the Exchange IIS app pool causes w3wp.exe to spawn cmd.exe/powershell.exe/net.exe/procdump.exe/7z.exe. The same tradecraft has been re-used by Silk Typhoon against Exchange/SharePoint and edge appliances through 2024-2025 (Microsoft cluster: Silk Typhoon, ex-HAFNIUM; Mandiant: UNC2639/UNC2640/UNC2643).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","makecab.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | eval is_lsass_dump=if(match(process,"(?i)(lsass|comsvcs\.dll.*MiniDump|-ma\s+lsass)"),1,0) | eval is_recon=if(match(process_name,"(?i)(net1?|whoami|quser|ipconfig|nltest|systeminfo|tasklist|ping|hostname|arp)\.exe"),1,0) | eval is_archive=if(match(process_name,"(?i)(7z|7za|makecab)\.exe"),1,0) | eval is_lolbin_download=if(match(process,"(?i)(certutil.*-urlcache|bitsadmin.*/transfer|Invoke-WebRequest|DownloadString|DownloadFile)"),1,0) | eval risk=is_lsass_dump*4+is_archive*2+is_recon+is_lolbin_download | where risk>=1 | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as drop_time values(Filesystem.file_path) as webshell_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as webshell_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asp") by host | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) ctime(drop_time) | sort - risk lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe (Exchange IIS app pool) spawning shell/recon/cred-dump LOLBins,
// optionally correlated with an .aspx webshell drop under Exchange virtual directories within 24h.
let Lookback = 30d;
let ExchangeWebPaths = dynamic([
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"
]);
let WebshellDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asp"
    | where FolderPath has_any (ExchangeWebPaths)
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              WebshellPath = strcat(FolderPath, FileName), WebshellSHA256 = SHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange", "Exchange")  // Exchange app pool, drop generic IIS noise
   or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "inetinfo.exe"
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\Exchange Server\"
| where FileName in~ (
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe",
    "nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe",
    "hostname.exe","arp.exe","dsquery.exe","adfind.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe",
    "makecab.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe",
    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe")
| extend IsLsassDump = ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"lsass", "comsvcs.dll MiniDump", "-ma lsass", "sekurlsa")
| extend IsArchive   = FileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe")
| extend IsRecon     = FileName in~ ("whoami.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","dsquery.exe","adfind.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe")
| extend IsLolbinDl  = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(certutil.*-urlcache|bitsadmin.*/transfer|Invoke-WebRequest|DownloadString|DownloadFile|wget |curl )"
| extend RiskScore   = toint(IsLsassDump)*4 + toint(IsArchive)*2 + toint(IsRecon) + toint(IsLolbinDl)
| where RiskScore >= 1
| join kind=leftouter (WebshellDrops) on DeviceId
| where isnull(DropTime) or Timestamp between (DropTime .. DropTime + 24h) or Timestamp between (DropTime - 1h .. DropTime + 24h)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsLsassDump, IsArchive, IsRecon, IsLolbinDl, RiskScore,
          WebshellPath, WebshellSHA256, DropTime
| order by RiskScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe spawning shell/recon/cred-dump LOLBins on Exchange (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let Lookback = 30d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildName) in (
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe",
    "nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe",
    "hostname.exe","arp.exe","dsquery.exe","adfind.exe",
    "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe",
    "makecab.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe",
    "certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","wmic.exe","schtasks.exe")
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend IsLsassDump = CommandLine has_any ("lsass", "comsvcs.dll MiniDump", "-ma lsass", "sekurlsa")
| extend IsArchive   = ChildName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe","makecab.exe")
| extend IsRecon     = ChildName in~ ("whoami.exe","quser.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","dsquery.exe","adfind.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe")
| extend IsLolbinDl  = CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(certutil.*-urlcache|bitsadmin.*/transfer|Invoke-WebRequest|DownloadString|DownloadFile|wget |curl )"
| extend RiskScore   = toint(IsLsassDump)*4 + toint(IsArchive)*2 + toint(IsRecon) + toint(IsLolbinDl)
| where RiskScore >= 1
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, IsLsassDump, IsArchive, IsRecon, IsLolbinDl, RiskScore
| order by RiskScore desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM Exchange w3wp.exe Suspicious Child Process
id: 8c5b1a73-2d4a-4e1d-9b1f-silk-typhoon-w3wp-child
status: stable
description: Detects w3wp.exe (the IIS application pool worker hosting Exchange OWA/ECP virtual directories) spawning shells, recon LOLBins, credential-dumping or archive utilities — the canonical post-exploitation chain Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) drops onto on-prem Exchange via a China-Chopper-style .aspx webshell after exploiting CVE-2021-26855 / 27065 and successor SSRF/auth-bypass bugs.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\dsquery.exe'
      - '\adfind.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
      - '\makecab.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  filter_legit:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_legit
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Exchange health probes / cumulative-update installers running w3wp child processes (rare, signed)
  - Legitimate Microsoft scripts (e.g. HealthChecker.ps1) spawned manually under w3wp
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe spawning a shell or recon LOLBin is the literal forensic artefact Microsoft and Volexity used to attribute the original ProxyLogon mass-exploitation campaign to HAFNIUM in March 2021, and the same parent-child relationship continues to appear in Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 intrusions against on-prem Exchange and SharePoint. Pairing it with a China-Chopper-style .aspx file written by w3wp.exe
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon mass mailbox exfiltration via Exchange Management Shell New-MailboxExportRequest
ucs.description: Hunts HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon's documented post-webshell collection step: loading the Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn (or running ExchangePowerShell cmdlets via the PSSession on a compromised Exchange CAS) and issuing New-MailboxExportRequest to dump targeted user mailboxes to .pst on a local/UNC staging path, often followed by 7z/makecab archiving for exfiltration. This was a defining HAFNIUM TTP called out by CISA AA21-062A and is still observed in Silk Typhoon's espionage operations against legal, defence-industrial-base and policy-research targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe") (Processes.process="*New-MailboxExportRequest*" OR Processes.process="*Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn*" OR Processes.process="*Add-PSSnapin*Exchange*" OR Processes.process="*New-MailboxSearch*" OR Processes.process="*Search-Mailbox*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | eval is_export = if(match(cmdline,"(?i)New-MailboxExportRequest"),1,0) | eval is_search = if(match(cmdline,"(?i)(New-MailboxSearch|Search-Mailbox.*-TargetMailbox|Search-Mailbox.*-DeleteContent)"),1,0) | eval pst_target = case(match(cmdline,"(?i)-FilePath\s+\\\\\\\\"),"unc-path",match(cmdline,"(?i)-FilePath\s+[A-Z]:\\\\(programdata|temp|users\\\\public|windows\\\\temp|inetpub)"),"suspicious-local",match(cmdline,"(?i)-FilePath"),"local",1=1,"none") | eval mailbox_arg = case(match(cmdline,"(?i)-Mailbox\s+\"?\*"),"wildcard-all",match(cmdline,"(?i)Get-Mailbox.*ResultSize\s+Unlimited"),"all-mailboxes",1=1,"specific") | where is_export=1 OR is_search=1 OR mailbox_arg!="specific" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange mailbox bulk-export via Exchange Management Shell
// (New-MailboxExportRequest / Search-Mailbox) — classic post-webshell collection step
let Lookback = 30d;
let ExchangeMgmtPatterns = dynamic([
    "New-MailboxExportRequest",
    "Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn",
    "Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.E2010",
    "Add-PSSnapin Microsoft.Exchange",
    "New-MailboxSearch",
    "Search-Mailbox",
    "Get-Mailbox"
]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (ExchangeMgmtPatterns)
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend IsExportRequest = Cmd has "new-mailboxexportrequest"
| extend IsBulkSearch    = Cmd has "new-mailboxsearch" or (Cmd has "search-mailbox" and (Cmd has "-targetmailbox" or Cmd has "-deletecontent"))
| extend IsBulkEnum      = Cmd has "get-mailbox" and (Cmd has "resultsize unlimited" or Cmd has "-resultsize unlimited")
| extend PstUncTarget    = Cmd matches regex @"(?i)-filepath\s+\\\\"  // UNC stage
| extend PstSuspLocal    = Cmd matches regex @"(?i)-filepath\s+[a-z]:\\(programdata|temp|users\\public|windows\\temp|inetpub|perflogs)"
| extend SpawnedFromW3wp = InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| extend RiskScore = toint(IsExportRequest)*3 + toint(IsBulkSearch)*2 + toint(IsBulkEnum) + toint(PstUncTarget)*2 + toint(PstSuspLocal)*2 + toint(SpawnedFromW3wp)*3
| where RiskScore >= 2
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          IsExportRequest, IsBulkSearch, IsBulkEnum,
          PstUncTarget, PstSuspLocal, SpawnedFromW3wp,
          RiskScore
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".pst" or FileName endswith ".zip" or FileName endswith ".7z" or FileName endswith ".cab"
    | summarize StagedFiles = make_set(strcat(FolderPath, FileName), 25),
                StageTime  = max(Timestamp)
              by DeviceName, DeviceId
  ) on DeviceName
| order by RiskScore desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange mailbox bulk-export via Exchange Management Shell
let Lookback = 30d;
let ExchangeMgmtPatterns = dynamic([
    "New-MailboxExportRequest",
    "Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.SnapIn",
    "Microsoft.Exchange.Management.PowerShell.E2010",
    "Add-PSSnapin Microsoft.Exchange",
    "New-MailboxSearch",
    "Search-Mailbox"
]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
    or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
    or NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell_ise.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (ExchangeMgmtPatterns)
| extend Cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| extend IsExportRequest = Cmd has "new-mailboxexportrequest"
| extend IsBulkSearch    = Cmd has "new-mailboxsearch" or (Cmd has "search-mailbox" and (Cmd has "-targetmailbox" or Cmd has "-deletecontent"))
| extend PstUncTarget    = Cmd matches regex @"(?i)-filepath\s+\\\\"
| extend PstSuspLocal    = Cmd matches regex @"(?i)-filepath\s+[a-z]:\\(programdata|temp|users\\public|windows\\temp|inetpub|perflogs)"
| extend SpawnedFromW3wp = ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend RiskScore = toint(IsExportRequest)*3 + toint(IsBulkSearch)*2 + toint(PstUncTarget)*2 + toint(PstSuspLocal)*2 + toint(SpawnedFromW3wp)*3
| where RiskScore >= 2
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName,
          IsExportRequest, IsBulkSearch, PstUncTarget, PstSuspLocal, SpawnedFromW3wp, RiskScore
| order by RiskScore desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: New-MailboxExportRequest abuse is one of the few HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon tradecraft markers that has been called out by name in CISA AA21-062A and Microsoft DART/MSTIC reporting — the actor uses it after webshell access to bulk-export targeted mailboxes to .pst, then archives them with 7z/makecab for exfiltration. The cmdlet is almost never invoked by ordinary admins on a compromised Exchange CAS, an
